response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and web engineer with nearly two decades of software development, but the role requires deep specialization in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his background. His performance-oriented work (low-latency DLang services, async I/O) is at the application/services layer, not the silicon or system-architecture level, and there is no evidence of deep-learning workload analysis or hardware modeling. This is a fundamental domain mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career (19+ years) spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded work
Genuine low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic (DLang, async I/O, fibers, high-throughput microservices)
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience whatsoever
No AI/deep-learning training or inference optimization background; no GPU or SIMD/MIMD architecture work
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from principal-level silicon performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DL workload experience
